prompt: We're going after six named enterprise accounts in financial services -- JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs, Citi, BNY Mellon, State Street, and Northern Trust. These are all existing customers on our mid-market...
assertions: Campaign brief contains all required sections: strategy rationale, audience definition, channel mix with budget allocation, lead magnet, sales-marketing integration, content calendar summary, success metrics, risk and contingency, budget summary
Strategy selection is ABM (account-based marketing) with rationale referencing named enterprise accounts, expansion deals, and high deal size -- not thought leadership or direct response
Budget allocation across all channels sums to exactly $120,000 with no unaccounted spend and no channel exceeding total budget
Primary KPI is pipeline-sourced and measurable (e.g., qualified pipeline dollar amount, expansion opportunities created) -- NOT impressions, reach, followers, or other vanity metrics

Sales-marketing integration section includes all four components: lead/account enrichment process, scoring model or engagement threshold for routing, sequence or play to deploy on engaged accounts, and SLA for AE response on hot accounts
Content calendar and production plan does not exceed capacity of one content marketer and a shared designer at 12 hours/week -- no section assumes resources that were not provided in the brief
